title: Architecture Explorer Document
description: Technology-agnostic description of a software system's modules, their connections, data flows, external dependencies, and end-to-end flows. Consumed by explorer.html (a generic recursive viewer). Designed to be produced by analyzing any codebase regardless of language or stack.

schemaVersion.description: Version of THIS schema the document targets.

meta.description: Top-level identity of the system being described.

subtitle.description: One-line positioning: what it is and the phase/scope.

description.description: 2–5 sentence prose overview of purpose, key constraints, and design philosophy.

tech.description: Primary stack (languages, frameworks, infra). Shown as pills in the header.

legend.description: The controlled vocabularies for node types and edge kinds, plus their display colors. node.type and connection.kind MUST reference ids defined here.

nodeTypes.description: The categories a module can have. Keep to ~6–12 meaningful, project-specific roles. Always include one for external systems.

edgeKinds.description: The kinds of relationships an arrow can represent (e.g. control/call, data flow, network, persistence, secret, device).

externals.description: Systems OUTSIDE this codebase that it talks to (databases, third-party APIs, OS services, message brokers, hardware). Rendered as dashed cards. Their ids may be referenced by any node's connection.to.

nodes.description: The root modules of the system (top level of the drill-down tree). Each node may contain children, recursively, to any depth.

flows.description: End-to-end sequences (e.g. request lifecycle, auth handshake, job pipeline). Each step references a node id so the viewer can jump to it.

id.description: Stable key referenced by node.type or connection.kind.

label.description: Human-readable name shown in the legend.

color.description: CSS color (hex recommended), used for the node dot / edge stroke.

node.description: One module at any level. A node can be a whole subsystem, a service/class, an external system, or a domain concept. Children let the same shape recurse.

id.description: Globally UNIQUE, stable, kebab-case id. Referenced by connection.to and flow step.node. Must be unique across nodes, children (any depth), and externals.

name.description: Display name (the real class/module/service name where possible).

type.description: One of legend.nodeTypes[].id. Drives the color. Unknown values fall back to a neutral color.

summary.description: 1–3 sentences: what this module is responsible for and WHY it exists. Grounded in the actual code, not generic boilerplate.

tags.description: Short keywords: cross-cutting properties, invariants, patterns, requirement ids. First 3 are shown on the card.

responsibilities.description: Bullet list of the concrete things this module does. Prefer verbs and real method/endpoint names.

keyFiles.description: Real source files that implement this node, with a short note. Paths MUST exist in the repo (relative to repo root).

connections.description: Directed relationships FROM this node TO another node/external. Represents real calls, data flows, or dependencies found in the code. Drives the arrows.

children.description: Sub-modules contained within this node. Recursing into a node shows these as its own diagram. Omit or leave empty for leaf nodes.

external.description: Optional explicit marker for an external system. Items under top-level `externals` are treated as external automatically.

connection.description: A directed edge. The arrow points from the owning node to `to`.

to.description: id of the target node or external. MUST resolve to some node.id / external.id somewhere in the document.

kind.description: One of legend.edgeKinds[].id. Drives the edge color and meaning.

label.description: Short verb phrase for the relationship (e.g. "claim / heartbeat", "reads token", "GET /api/...").

payload.description: What actually flows across this edge: the data shape, message, protocol detail, or call semantics.

summary.description: One line on what the flow accomplishes and any key invariant.

steps.description: Ordered steps. Each names the node that acts and what it does.

node.description: id of the node performing this step (must resolve).

action.description: What happens at this step, grounded in the code.
